text: This exercise is camera zero. It is a simulated exercise to focus on coordination and camera movements.

camera zero should be considered more of an orientation exercise rather than something that requires a lot of time to achieve a high score on as it is fairly easy to do well in this exercise, even on the first try.
And it provides good practice with coordinating the arms, as well as getting to understand the console and how the camera works.
You'll be required to move the camera all around the field in order to find floating spheres that you will need to zoom in on and center on in order to move on to the next sphere.
Take note to the arrow pointing to your interface indicating the orientation of your camera. This will help you keep the camera as level as possible during the exercise.

Moving around the field is done by coordinating left and right arm and holding down on the camera foot pedal with your left foot. It's important to note that when you do this, you want to keep your right and left arm in the field of view at all times and clutching your instrument as needed.
The green light activates, indicating an optimal view once the sphere is centered and appropriately zoomed in using the camera.
The flashing yellow arrow directs the camera towards the next sphere, which becomes visible as the operator adjusts the zoom to achieve an optimal view, indicated by the green light.
Again, this is not a difficult exercise to achieve a high score on. Just make sure that you keep your instruments in field of view and coordinate appropriately with the left foot camera pedal.
